text: CHART OF THE DAY: The history of China's consumption of fossil fuels, from 1965 to 2025. Although as a share of total energy, fossil fuels has seen a decline, in absolute terms (in tonnes of coal, barrels of oil, and cubic meters of gas) demand set an all-time high last year. https://t.co/rKix4LANRL
@takis2910 Absolutely. Two things could be true at the same time: China is a leader in green energy; and the world’s largest fossil fuel consumer.
In a statement, Iran said its foreign minister met with his Omani counterpart today and “exchanged views on appropriate mechanisms for the safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz.”

Iran said Saturday that it’s closing the Strait of Hormuz, saying no vessels will be allowed to pass through until foreign interference ends, according state-run IRIB News.

The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps also said it fired a warning shot on a cargo ship that tried to transit the strait on Saturday despite warnings, the outlet added.
